data.title: Fed's Lisa Cook warns of high inflation, 'K-shaped' economy leaving low-income Americans behind. PCE inflation at 2.9%. Wait and see approach on rates.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook spoke at the Economic Club of Miami, highlighting a "wait and see" approach to monetary policy. She described current interest rates as "mildly restrictive" and expressed concern over a "K-shaped" economic divergence disadvantaging low-income families. Cook supported the FOMC's decision to hold the policy rate steady, noting a disinflation period between 2022-2024, which stalled in 2025. PCE inflation stood at 2.9%, above the Fed's 2% target, attributed to core goods prices rising post-tariff increases. Cook stressed the need for stronger evidence of inflation moving sustainably down to target, focusing on maintaining credibility to avoid entrenched higher inflation. The full story can be read on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Stock futures fluctuate after Trump ends shutdown, private sector hiring slows with only 22,000 jobs added. AMD down 7.63%, SMCI up 12.27%. Indices mixed: Dow +0.16%, S&P +0.07%, Nasdaq -0.28%. 10-year bond yield at 4.27%, 2-year at 3.58%. Fed expected to maintain interest rates in March.
data.description: On Wednesday, U.S. stock futures were volatile following a negative close the previous day. The ADP's January employment report revealed a significant slowdown in U.S. private sector hiring, with only 22,000 jobs added, signaling a three-year trend of diminishing job creation. Despite this, wage growth remained robust, with annual pay for existing employees rising by 4.5%. President Trump signed a funding deal to end a brief government shutdown, impacting economic indicators. The 10-year Treasury bond yield was at 4.27%, while the two-year bond stood at 3.58%. Markets predict a 91.1% chance of the Federal Reserve maintaining interest rates in March. Index performance saw the Dow Jones increase by 0.16%, S&P 500 by 0.07%, Nasdaq 100 drop by 0.28%, and Russell 2000 rise by 0.31%. The SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust was up 0.13% at $690.41, while the Invesco QQQ Trust ETF fell by 0.24% to $615.01 in premarket trading. Advanced Micro Devices stock declined by 7.63% despite strong Q4 results, while Super Micro Computer surged by 12.27%.

data.title: Simple AI secures $14M investment from First Harmonic and Y Combinator to develop Voice AI agents for sales.
data.description: In San Francisco, Simple AI, a platform for voice AI agents, revealed today that it secured $14 million in a seed funding round. The investment was spearheaded by First Harmonic, along with contributions from key players like Y Combinator, Massive Tech Ventures, and True Ventures. The funding will be channeled towards enhancing their voice agent platform, developing bespoke generative AI models, and expanding analytics and customer insights services for businesses of varying scales. Simple AI's advanced voice AI technology powers their agents, offering automation for inbound calls.

data.title: Asia Pacific Retail Pharmacy Market to reach USD 524.8B by 2032, up from USD 344.9B in 2024 - Credence Research.
data.description: A recent market study by Credence Research Inc. has shed light on the promising future of the Asia Pacific Retail Pharmacy Market. With a standout projection, the market is anticipated to escalate from a substantial value of USD 344.9 Billion in 2024 to a remarkable USD 524.8 Billion by 2032. This growth trajectory underscores significant opportunities and potential for expansion within the industry.

data.title: VEON Ltd. notes successful Kyivstar secondary offering of 14,375,000 shares at $10.50 each, oversubscribed 5 times.
data.description: VEON Ltd. announced the completion of a secondary public offering for Kyivstar Group Ltd., Ukraine's top digital operator, on February 2, 2026. The offering consisted of 14,375,000 common shares priced at USD 10.50 per share. This offering was oversubscribed by 5 times. The sale included shares from VEON Amsterdam B.V., the main shareholder, as well as 400,000 common shares from other selling shareholders.
Ongwe Minerals has completed reverse takeover and financing, trading to start soon, with key figures undisclosed.

data.title: Dono raises $6.5M seed round, totaling $10.2M in funding, to modernize U.S. property records. Major asset class valued at $50 trillion.
data.description: Dono, a property records platform utilizing AI technology to convert scattered county records into comprehensive ownership data, has disclosed a successful $6.5 million seed funding round, bringing its total funding to $10.2 million. Link Ventures spearheaded the round, joined by lool VC and Alumni Ventures. The vast U.S. asset class, valued at more than $50 trillion, relies heavily on obsolete paper records that cannot be easily accessed or searched. This innovative solution aims to address this problem.

data.title: pHathom Technologies raises over $12M in committed capital after $4M seed financing round.
data.description: pHathom Technologies, based in Halifax, Nova Scotia, has raised over $12 million in total committed capital after completing a successful $4 million seed round.

data.title: Yoroll.ai is developing the first engine-less game platform in San Francisco, utilizing AI technology.
data.description: In San Francisco, a revolutionary shift is taking place in the world of gaming as Yoroll.ai unveils the first engine-less game platform. This innovative venture marks the beginning of the World Model Revolution in the industry. By eliminating the need for traditional engines, Yoroll.ai is set to disrupt the gaming landscape. Stay tuned for further developments in this groundbreaking initiative.

data.title: GENFIT to receive US$20M milestone from Ipsen as Iqirvo® surpasses US$200M in first year sales on PBC market.
data.description: In Lille, France, Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States, and Zurich, Switzerland, on February 12, 2026, GENFIT (Euronext: GNFT), a biopharmaceutical company committed to enhancing the well-being of patients with rare and severe liver ailments, revealed that the exceptional commercial success of Iqirvo® in its initial complete year on the primary biliary cholangitis (PBC) market has led to a significant US$20 million milestone payment from Ipsen as per their licensing deal.

data.title: Wabtec beats Q4 expectations with $2.10 EPS and $2.965B sales. FY2026 forecast: $10.05-$10.45 EPS, $12.190B-$12.490B sales. Analysts raise estimates.
data.description: Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies Corp (NYSE: WAB) exceeded expectations with their fourth quarter earnings report. They reported quarterly earnings of $2.10 per share, surpassing the analyst consensus of $2.08 per share. The company's quarterly sales totaled $2.965 billion, also beating the estimated $2.864 billion. For FY2026, Westinghouse Air Brake anticipates an adjusted EPS of $10.05-$10.45, as compared to market projections of $10.23. Sales for the fiscal year are expected to fall within the range of $12.190 billion-$12.490 billion, exceeding estimates of $11.977 billion. The company is pleased with the solid performance in the fourth quarter and for the full year. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Correction made to news release by Ramp about launch of Accounting Agent to automate bookkeeping with real-time close on 12-Feb-2026.
data.description: Ramp has announced the launch of Accounting Agent on 12-Feb-2026, to streamline bookkeeping processes with a real-time close. The company revealed that the initial news, disseminated by Ramp through PR Newswire, was inaccurate due to transmission errors.

data.title: Houston-based EWR Digital acquires Modalpoint to strengthen oil & gas marketing authority, founded by Mark LaCour.
data.description: On February 12, 2026, EWR Digital, an energy and industrial marketing company located in Houston, revealed the purchase of Modalpoint, a well-regarded brand in the oil & gas market insight and commercialization strategy sector. Modalpoint was established by industry expert Mark LaCour.
